channel.title: Github on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Github on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Easily apply Copilot code review feedback with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub renamed Copilot code review’s “Implement suggestion” button to “Fix with Copilot”. The updated flow now includes a UI dialog for more control when applying suggestions with the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Expanded OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for organization-level private registries on Cloudsmith and Google Artifact Registry.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Removal of code_scanning_upload field from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub removed the code_scanning_upload field from the REST API rate_limit endpoint response as of May 19, 2026. The field no longer appears in the resources object.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Audit repository Copilot cloud agent configuration via the REST API
item.description:
GitHub added a public preview REST API to get a repository’s Copilot cloud agent configuration. This lets you audit that configuration programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Spaces API now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Spaces API is now generally available. It supports programmatic create, read, update, and delete operations for Spaces from external applications.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Grok Code Fast 1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Team-level Copilot usage metrics now available via API
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics API now includes a new user-teams report that maps each Copilot-licensed user to the teams they belong to. This can be joined with existing usage reports to analyze Copilot usage at the team level.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app is now available in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is available in technical preview as a GitHub-native desktop experience for starting agentic development from existing work while keeping it isolated and steerable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New enterprise installation API now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has introduced a new Enterprise Installation API in public preview for GitHub App developers. The API fills a gap identified from community feedback and lets an app determine whether it is installed in an enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now start Copilot cloud agent tasks programmatically using a new Agent tasks REST API. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot code review comments in the new pull requests experience to make them easier to scan and act on. The changes are available to all users opted into that experience and include grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated comment layout.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description:
GitHub now lets customers download April usage reports to see how Copilot activity maps to AI credits ahead of the new usage-based billing rollout. The billing unit of AI credits is going live on a staged schedule.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Synchronous SBOM API deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated the older synchronous SBOM REST API after releasing a new asynchronous SBOM API. The synchronous API is scheduled for removal on November 13, 2026, giving a six-month migration window.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cross-org Dependabot access for internal repositories
item.description:
Dependabot can now access internal repositories in other organizations within the same enterprise. This lets Dependabot resolve dependencies hosted in cross-org internal GitHub repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now reports Copilot code review suggestions broken down by comment type. This adds a new copilot_suggestions_by_comment_type metric for analyzing code review activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments on the user level
item.description:
GitHub now lets personal account owners set commit comments to be enabled or disabled by default for their repositories. This adds a user-level default for commit comment behavior across repos they own.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions concurrency groups now allow larger queues
item.description:
You can now allow multiple jobs or workflow runs to wait in the same GitHub Actions concurrency group instead of being limited to a single pending run. Previously, a concurrency…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview
item.description:
Enterprise Live Migrations (ELM) is now in public preview. It lets enterprise administrators migrate repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) to GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository rulesets: User bypass and branch renaming
item.description:
GitHub repository rulesets now allow individual users to be added as bypass actors. Organization rulesets also now support renaming branches that they cover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed plugins in GitHub Copilot CLI are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports enterprise-managed plugins in public preview. Enterprise admins can configure and distribute plugins to Copilot CLI users across the organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Search and filter bar for repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added a search bar and filters to the Security tab’s repository security advisories list. This lets you search and narrow advisories directly within a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning in the GitHub MCP server is now generally available. It can be used with MCP-compatible AI coding agents or IDEs, including GitHub Copilot CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says the code-to-cloud risk visibility integration with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available. The release includes follow-up improvements since public preview, bringing artifact and runtime context closer together.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation notice: code_scanning_upload field will be removed from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub will remove the code_scanning_upload field from the rate_limit REST API endpoint response on May 19, 2026. The notice says the field is being deprecated and will no longer be returned after that date.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Student GPT-5.3-Codex removal from model picker
item.description:
GitHub is removing GPT-5.3-Codex from the Copilot Student plan’s model picker starting today. The model is still available when using auto model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to notification retention and archived repository watches
item.description:
GitHub is changing how long notifications are retained and how watches on archived repositories are handled. The rollout is expected to finish over the next few months.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
item.description:
GitHub is moving the new global pull requests dashboard to an opt-out public preview, so it will be enabled by default for all users. The new dashboard and inbox experience is transitioning from opt-in to opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics
item.description:
GitHub added a new used_copilot_cloud_agent boolean field to the Copilot usage metrics API in user-level reports. It mirrors the existing Copilot coding agent field after the rename to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python
item.description:
GitHub now uses Dependabot-based data to generate more complete and accurate transitive dependency trees for Python dependency graphs and SBOMs. This change improves coverage for Python projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments across your organization
item.description:
GitHub now lets organization owners disable commit comments at the org level, removing the need to change each repository individually. This follows a prior repo-level option to disable commit comments.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View and manage agent sessions from issues and projects
item.description:
GitHub now lets you view and steer cloud agent sessions directly from issues and projects. This adds visibility into agent activity without leaving the current workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business for some organizations. The change is described as part of ongoing efforts to keep the Copilot experience reliable and sustainable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming change to Copilot usage metrics report download URLs
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot usage metrics report download links from Azure Front Door domains to a stable GitHub-owned custom domain. The update is intended to improve URL stability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review user counts now aggregate in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now aggregates Copilot code review active and passive user counts in enterprise and organization usage reports. This follows the launch of active and passive user identification for Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
We have deprecated the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled GitHub Code Security configurations now…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Sunsetting SHA-1 in HTTPS on GitHub
item.description:
GitHub says it will remove SHA-1 use in HTTPS for GitHub and its CDNs. This change affects browsers and other software that connect to or view GitHub over HTTPS.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI supports custom registry based MCP allowlists
item.description:
Enterprise and organization administrators can now bring their own MCP registry and enforce allowlist policies in Copilot CLI. This feature is in public preview and brings MCP governance controls to…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Rule insights dashboard and unified filter bar
item.description:
GitHub announced a new rule insights dashboard and a unified filter bar for repository rulesets. The update is meant to make it easier to spot trends such as blocked-push spikes and bypass patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for private registries configured at the organization level. This removes the need to store long-lived credentials as repository secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deployment context in repository properties and alerts
item.description:
GitHub added deployment context to repository properties and security alert pages. Two new built-in repository properties, deployable and deployed, now expose this information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues
item.description:
GitHub added the ability to link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues. The feature is in public preview and is meant to connect security remediation work with existing planning and tracking workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot and code scanning: Org-level private registries
item.description:
GitHub now lets organizations configure multiple private registries at the org level for Dependabot and code scanning, which is useful for setups that use more than one internal package feed. Previously, org-level settings supported only one private registry per ecosystem.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates and product improvements
item.description:
GitHub says it is rolling out secret scanning updates that improve detection coverage, APIs, and related workflows. The post says these changes are part of ongoing secret scanning product improvements, but the provided excerpt does not include具体 details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: SBOM exports are now computed asynchronously
item.description:
SBOM exports from repository dependency graph pages and the new API endpoints are now computed asynchronously. This changes export handling from a synchronous action to an async one.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Model selection for Claude and Codex agents on github.com
item.description:
GitHub.com now lets users select the model when starting Claude and Codex third-party coding agent runs. The feature is available for these agents, similar to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fix merge conflicts in three clicks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added a new “Fix with Copilot” button on github.com for resolving merge conflicts, powered by Copilot cloud agent. The feature lets users fix conflicts from a comment flow in three clicks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description:
GitHub is enforcing new usage limits for Copilot Pro+ and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from that plan. The change is intended to address high-concurrency and heavy-usage patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description:
GitHub is renaming Copilot coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and updating the data schema to reflect that change. Copilot usage metrics will now aggregate active user counts for the cloud agent under the new naming.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro free trials because of increased abuse of the trial system. The change is intended to protect platform integrity for legitimate developers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include CLI activity in the overall totals and feature breakdowns. Previously, CLI metrics were only available separately in a standalone totals_by_cli section.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description:
GitHub says the validation tools used by Copilot cloud agent are now 20% faster. The tools include CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret scanning, and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description:
GitHub added an Ask Copilot entry point in security assessment results, letting organization admins and security managers get contextual explanations and next-step guidance directly from secret risk assessment and Code Security risk assessment views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description:
GitHub moderators can now mark hidden comments as Low Quality from the Hide comment menu. The option is available on issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description:
GitHub Notifications now includes a new “Sort by” control with two options: “Newest to oldest” and “Oldest to newest.” The default behavior remains “Newest to oldest.”

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description:
GitHub now shows repository member role labels inline in the pull request list view for public repositories. Labels such as First-time contributor, Contributor, and Member appear directly in the list.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description:
GitHub added release tracking details to the issue sidebar, configurable default values for project fields, and faster navigation between related issues.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes two new merge metrics for Copilot-reviewed pull requests, extending the pull request throughput and cycle-time metrics added in February. These metrics track PRs reviewed by Copilot from the coding agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out updates to secret scanning alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows. The changes are aimed at improving the developer experience for secret scanning features.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description:
Organization admins and security managers can now run a free Code Security risk assessment for their organization. It summarizes vulnerabilities by severity, rule type, and related categories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description:
Dependabot alerts can now be assigned to AI coding agents for remediation, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex. This is intended for vulnerabilities that require code changes beyond a simple dependency version update.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem via Nix flakes. You can add nix as a package ecosystem in dependabot.yml and Dependabot will monitor flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when updates are available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests can now be fixed in bulk from the Files changed tab. The update lets users apply multiple security alert suggestions at once instead of handling them individually.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot code review activity and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can use these metrics to see how users engage with Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent. The built-in agent firewall can now be configured centrally to control internet access and help reduce prompt injection and data exfiltration risk.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent signs its commits
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now signs every commit it creates. These commits show up as Verified on GitHub, indicating they were made by the agent and not altered after signing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now in public preview. It provides building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities into applications, workflows, and platform services.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description:
GitHub has renamed the top-level Security tab to Security & quality across repositories, organizations, and enterprises on github.com. The navigation now groups code quality findings together with security-related information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says improved search for Issues is now generally available. It was previously in public preview and later added to the Issues dashboard.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent, formerly Copilot coding agent, is no longer limited to pull-request workflows. This expands how it can be used for research, planning, and coding tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub secret scanning — coverage update
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning added nine new secret detectors from seven providers as part of a coverage update. The update includes detectors for providers such as Langchain, Salesforce, and Figma, along with additional secret types from Figma, Google, and OpenVSX.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL pull requests insights on security overview now cover all protected branches
item.description:
CodeQL pull request insights in GitHub Security Overview now include Copilot Autofix and alert statistics from all protected branches, instead of only the default branch.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Credential revocation API now supports GitHub OAuth and GitHub app credentials
item.description:
GitHub extended the Credential revocation API to support GitHub OAuth and GitHub App credentials. The API can now be used to programmatically revoke exposed credentials found in repositories or elsewhere.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to resolve merge conflicts on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot coding agent can now resolve merge conflicts on pull requests. Users can trigger it by mentioning @copilot in a PR comment and asking it to handle the conflicts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New pull requests dashboard is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has released a public preview of a refreshed pull requests dashboard at github.com/pulls. It adds a new pull request inbox and saved views for organizing and prioritizing pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agent activity in GitHub Issues and Projects
item.description:
GitHub added support for surfacing coding agent sessions in Issues and Projects, so agent activity can be tracked directly in planning workflows. The update includes agent sessions shown under assignees in GitHub Issues and agent activity integration in GitHub Projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to our Privacy Statement and Terms of Service: How we use your data
item.description:
GitHub updated its Privacy Statement and Terms of Service to clarify how it handles user data. The changes take effect on April 24.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable comments on individual commits
item.description:
Repository admins can now disable comments on individual commits. GitHub added a new “Commits” section in repository settings to manage this behavior.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
On April 21, 2026, we’re deprecating and removing the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to make changes to any pull request
item.description:
GitHub now lets you mention @copilot in any pull request to request changes directly in the PR. One announced use case is fixing failing GitHub Actions workflows and tests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage Copilot coding agent repository access via the API
item.description:
GitHub introduced public preview REST APIs for managing Copilot coding agent repository access programmatically at the organization level. This lets organization owners control access at scale through the API instead of only manual settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Faster incremental analysis with CodeQL in pull requests
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C#, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, and Ruby are now incremental, which improves scan speed. GitHub had already sped up pull request scans earlier this year.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Push protection exemptions from repository settings
item.description:
GitHub now lets repository admins manage secret scanning push protection exemptions from a repository’s settings. Before this change, exemptions were only configurable through organization- and enterprise-level security configurations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Trace any Copilot coding agent commit to its session logs
item.description:
GitHub says commits made by Copilot coding agent are authored by Copilot with the assigning human listed as co-author, and that these commits can now be traced back to the agent session logs. This is meant to make it easier to inspect how a given commit was produced.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More visibility into Copilot coding agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub added session log visibility for Copilot coding agent tasks. You can now review what the agent did while it worked in the background before approving its changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster. The update applies when handing work to the agent through supported entry points such as assigning an issue to Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out docked panels on pull request “Files changed” pages so reviewers can keep pull request context open side-by-side while reviewing code. The panels include overview, comments, and merge status.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now automatically runs project tests and linting, plus GitHub validation tools such as CodeQL, advisory database checks, and secret scanning. The new update lets you configure which validation tools the agent uses.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent works faster with semantic code search
item.description:
Copilot coding agent now supports a semantic code search tool, so it can find relevant code by meaning instead of exact text matches. GitHub says this should make the agent faster when working in codebases.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now detects malware in npm dependencies
item.description:
Dependabot can now alert on npm dependencies that match known malware advisories. When malware alerting is enabled, it checks repository npm dependencies against malicious package versions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning in AI coding agents via the GitHub MCP Server
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server can now scan code changes for exposed secrets before commit or pull request creation. The feature is intended to catch credential leaks early in AI coding agent workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Push protection exemptions for roles, teams, and apps
item.description:
GitHub added push protection exemptions for secret scanning at the organization level. Specific roles, teams, and GitHub Apps can now be exempted, with exemption status checked on each push.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code Quality permissions removed from security manager role
item.description:
GitHub changed repository permissions for Code Quality: security managers can no longer enable or disable GitHub Code Quality unless they are also repository administrators. The ability to toggle Code Quality is now limited to repository admins.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Batch apply quality suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality findings on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for Code Quality findings in the Files changed tab by…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page
item.description:
We are deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page and in the People/Licensing CSV export. When the cost center integration was originally shipped in 2023, cost center…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Optionally skip approval for Copilot coding agent Actions workflows
item.description:
When Copilot coding agent opens a pull request or pushes changes, Copilot is treated like an outside contributor in an open source project. GitHub Actions workflows do not run until…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Self-hosted runner minimum version enforcement paused
item.description:
Editor’s note (March 17, 2026): We updated this post to clarify that the v2.329.0 minimum version enforcement that has been paused applies only at configuration/registration time (i.e., when running ./config.sh…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot for students
item.description:
Starting today, students with GitHub Education benefits are now on the new GitHub Copilot Student plan. As part of this transition, we’ve updated the available model lineup. The new GitHub…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: REST API version 2026-03-10 is now available
item.description:
Previously, we introduced calendar-based versioning for our REST API, giving us a path to evolving our API while giving integrators plenty of time and clear guidance for upgrading. Now, we’re…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot auto model selection is generally available in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in JetBrains IDEs for all Copilot plans. With auto, Copilot chooses a model on your behalf based on real-time model availability…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions OIDC tokens now support repository custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Actions OpenID Connect (OIDC) tokens now support repository custom properties as claims. Additionally, a new settings page is available in public preview, making it easy to configure OIDC token…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Issue fields: Structured issue metadata is in public preview
item.description:
Issue fields are now available in public preview for select GitHub organizations. If you’ve been using labels like priority/p0 or severity/high to track structured data in issues, you know the…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Request Copilot code review from GitHub CLI
item.description:
You can now request a review from GitHub Copilot directly from your terminal using the GitHub CLI. Whether you’re editing an existing pull request or creating a new one, Copilot…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Major agentic capabilities improvements in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
This update brings several new features and improvements to GitHub Copilot in JetBrains IDEs. Core agentic capabilities, including custom agents, sub-agents, and plan agent, are now generally available, with agent…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Explore a repository using Copilot on the web
item.description:
We’ve made it easier to explore a repository’s file structure when you chat with Copilot on the web. What’s changed Developers can now ask Copilot to show a file, then…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates — March 2026
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning continually updates its detectors, validators, and analyzers. Here’s what’s new for March 2026. 28 new secret detectors from 15 providers, including Lark, Vercel, Snowflake, and Supabase. 39…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.24.3 adds Java 26 support and other improvements
item.description:
CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.24.3, which adds support for Java 26…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now supports pre-commit hooks
item.description:
GitHub Dependabot now natively supports automatic dependency updates for pre-commit hooks. By adding pre-commit as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml configuration, Dependabot will parse your .pre-commit-config.yaml, check each hook’s…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Extend your coding agent with .NET Skills
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the dotnet/skills repository, describing a set of .NET “agent skills” intended to extend coding agents and improve agent-driven development workflows.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Discover and manage agent activity with new session filters
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise AI Controls and the agent control plane add new session filters to help enterprises find and manage agent activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pick a model for @copilot in pull request comments
item.description:
GitHub Copilot’s coding agent can be invoked in pull request comments via @copilot, and the feature adds the ability to choose which model @copilot uses for that request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot coding agent can now be assigned Jira issues to asynchronously generate draft pull requests in a linked GitHub repository. This integration is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review now runs on an agentic architecture
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review has moved to an agentic, tool-calling architecture and is now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Quick access to merge status in pull requests is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out a merge-status indicator at the top of pull request pages, now in public preview. This lets reviewers and authors check merge readiness from anywhere in the PR view.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Add images to agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub agent sessions now support starting from an image by adding it directly in the agent UI on github.com.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes user-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include user-level activity for GitHub Copilot CLI, expanding beyond prior enterprise-level CLI telemetry.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — February update
item.description:
The February update to Visual Studio 2026 brings expanded agent capabilities, smarter code completions, and deeper Copilot integration across debugging, testing, and modernization workflows in Visual Studio. Highlights Here’s what’s…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Lock and unlock draft repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added the ability for repository administrators to lock draft repository security advisories and private vulnerability reports to stop collaborators from editing them.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory now on by default for Pro and Pro+ users in public preview
item.description:
Copilot Memory is now enabled by default for GitHub Copilot Pro and Pro+ users in public preview, instead of being opt-in.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot alert assignees are now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Dependabot now supports assigning vulnerability alerts to specific users (generally available). This adds per-alert ownership to help teams track and remediate dependency vulnerabilities.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality enterprise policy
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security enterprise policies now allow configuring GitHub Code Quality availability independently from Code Security.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Email notifications for included usage thresholds
item.description:
GitHub now sends email alerts as an account’s included monthly usage approaches its thresholds.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Network configuration changes for Copilot coding agent now in effect
item.description:
GitHub has put into effect (as of Feb 27, 2026) previously announced required network configuration changes for teams running Copilot coding agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot metrics now includes plan mode
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include telemetry for plan mode. Enterprises can track plan mode adoption and engagement alongside existing Copilot metrics.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes enterprise-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot enterprise usage metrics now include GitHub Copilot CLI telemetry, expanding what’s counted in enterprise-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot metrics is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics are now generally available, providing a centralized view of team adoption and usage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot metrics report URLs update
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics API download URLs are now served from a new endpoint; report data, API contract, and response schema are unchanged.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise AI Controls & agent control plane now generally available
item.description:
GitHub announced general availability of Enterprise AI Controls and the agent control plane for GitHub Enterprise administrators.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Content Exclusion REST API in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot adds a new Content Exclusion REST API (JSON) in public preview for organization and enterprise admins to manage Copilot content exclusion rules programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved search on the issues dashboard
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out semantic (natural-language) search in public preview on the Issues dashboard over the coming week.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Org-level metrics API now includes pull request throughput metric parity
item.description:
GitHub expanded the organization-level Copilot Usage Metrics API to add pull request activity metrics, bringing the org-level endpoints closer to other metrics parity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Generate pull request titles with Copilot on the web
item.description:
GitHub Copilot can now generate pull request titles directly on github.com when creating or editing a PR.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now available in github.com, GitHub Mobile, and Visual Studio
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is generally available for GitHub Copilot paid tiers and can be used via Copilot Chat across github.com, GitHub Mobile, and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved web search in Copilot on github.com
item.description:
Copilot Chat on github.com now supports model-native web search for certain models, allowing those models to use their built-in web browsing capability during chats.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot CLI is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI is now generally available for all paid GitHub Copilot subscribers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 release candidate is available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 release candidate is available. The changelog post indicates improvements in deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, but provides no concrete details in the provided excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot can group updates by dependency name across multiple directories
item.description:
Dependabot can now group update PRs by dependency name across multiple directories in a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Organization-level dashboard in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality added an organization-level dashboard in public preview to view code health across repositories with Code Quality enabled.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository dashboard is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub’s repository dashboard has moved from public preview to general availability. It lets users find, filter, and save custom views of repositories they can access.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-defined custom organization roles are generally available
item.description:
GitHub now generally makes available enterprise-defined custom organization roles to help enterprises standardize role-based access across multiple organizations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise team support in organization APIs
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise organization owners can now discover and manage both enterprise teams and organization teams via existing organization team API endpoints.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization-level Copilot usage metrics dashboard available in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has added an organization-level GitHub Copilot usage metrics dashboard in public preview, expanding access beyond enterprise-only dashboards.

  • Source: GitHub
